case_name: 1.	This is an application to commit the Respondent for contempt of court on the grounds of making knowingly false statements in documents supported by statements of truth. The criminal standard of proof applies. That means that the application cannot succeed unless, having considered the evidence, I am satisfied so that I am sure that (1) one or more of the statements was false, (2) that such statement has, or if persisted in would be likely to have, interfered with the course of justice in some material respects, and (3) that at the time it was made, the Respondent had no honest belief in the truth of the statement and knew of its likelihood to interfere with the course of justice. That test was set out inAXA Insurance UK Plc v Rossiter[2013] EWHC 3805 QBat [9].

The criminal standard of proof applies. That means that the application cannot succeed unless, having considered the evidence, I am satisfied so that I am sure that (1) one or more of the statements was false, (2) that such statement has, or if persisted in would be likely to have, interfered with the course of justice in some

evidence_index.text: Megan was born on 7 July 2000 with severe acetabular dysplasia, which is a severe disorder of the hip joints.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Applicant did however admit that there were some breaches of duty in connection with surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Dwyer found that 'her symptomatology is confusing in that she appears to have neuralgic pain most probably attributable to multiple surgeries and sensitisation of a lateral cutaneous nerve of thighs'.
evidence_index.speaker: Mr Dwyer, Orthopaedic Surgeon

evidence_index.text: Professor Montgomery also felt that many of Megan's reported symptoms could not have been caused by her hip problems and that one possible explanation for them was exaggeration, conscious or unconscious.
evidence_index.speaker: Professor Montgomery, Orthopaedic Expert

evidence_index.text: Ms Bull did not observe her having any difficulty in walking. She did not recall her ever using a wheelchair and was sure that she would remember if she had seen this.
evidence_index.speaker: Ellie Bull, History Teacher

evidence_index.text: I therefore find that the statements specified in paragraphs 104-105 above were false and that Mrs Colley knew that they were false when she made them.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The deception may have prolonged the litigation and increased the costs but I cannot be sure that it did, given the other fundamental issues in play.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The sentence is imprisonment for 6 months, suspended for 2 years on condition that no other contempt of court is committed during that time.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Chronic hip pain and mobility issues
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports chronic hip pain, difficulty walking, and reliance on wheelchair for long distances.

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with chronic hip pain and reported mobility issues
decision_points.prompt: What should be the approach to assess the patient's functional capacity?

options.clinical_reasoning: Provides objective assessment of mobility and functional limitations

options.description: Rely on patient and family reports
options.clinical_reasoning: Subjective reports may not accurately reflect functional capacity

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on patient and family reports
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant accepted reported limitations without objective assessment

expected_action_court.description: Functional capacity evaluation should have been conducted
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Objective assessment needed to verify reported limitations

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to objectively assess functional capacity led to reliance on potentially exaggerated reports
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Functional capacity evaluation would have provided objective data

patient_outcome.description: Patient's claims of disability found to be exaggerated
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Potential psychological impact due to litigation

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to verify claims with objective evidence
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Objective assessment would have clarified true capabilities
